data.title: Blue Lagoon Jumps 50% With Transition To Gold Production
data.description:
Blue Lagoon Resources Inc. (BLAGF) rose nearly 50% since the beginning of 2026. This move was driven by the company announcing it received its first revenues. In business life cycles, first revenues are an important derisking milestone for a company. For a gold mining company, it is especially important because the company's reliance on outside capital has largely been eliminated and its operating prospects should improve significantly. Though the 970,000 Canadian dollars payment is merely an advance on the current 1000 tons of ore Blue Lagoon first produced, we anticipate that the actual milling results and production rates will be disclosed in the coming days and weeks. Furthermore, those results should begin to point toward a cash flow rate of $2-3 million per month in 2026. We estimate 2026 cash flows on the order of $30 million, and at a market cap to cash flow multiple of 7 times, BLAGF's market cap should approach $210 million, or $1.5/share on 140 million shares this year.

The Lottery Ticket:

Income Growth Advisors, LLC, owns 5.8 million shares of Blue Lagoon Resources, Inc., and is biased and bullish on the company's prospects. Our hopes are inspired by Blue Lagoon's massive 18-kilometer unexplored alkaline quartz resource package in safe and beautiful British Columbia, Canada, and four expert geological perspectives:

  • The link to the interview above, with Crescat Capital's world-renowned geologist Quinton Hennigh, PhD, in Deepdive.ca in February 2022, inspired our hopes for a massive resource at BLAGF. Crescat owns just under 10% of BLAGF shares and has worked closely with BLAGF on developing this resource opportunity. Crescat paid as much as $0.72/share and continued to accumulate shares down to $0.15/share through 2025.
  • Nicola Mining Inc. (HUSIF) owns 7% of Blue Lagoon's shares. Nicola's investment in Blue Lagoon is especially reassuring because it processed a 5000-ton sample of BLAGF ore in 2022 and was paid $1.6 million when gold was trading at $1800/ounce. Nicola has added to its equity investments following its sample run, extended its milling agreement to 10 years, extended an unsecured line of credit for $2mm and HUSIF CEO Peter Espig has personally invested in the company.
  • IBK Capital is another sophisticated precious metals resource investor in Blue Lagoon and sees district-scale potential for BLAGF. IBK Capital is formerly Merrill Lynch's Canadian Resource Investment Bank. Its President and CEO, Michael White, is a three-decade resource investment banker who helped raise Blue Lagoon's early capital and invested directly, and for its clients, in BLAGF.
  • In September 2025, at the Precious Metals Summit at Beaver Creek, CO, thirty-three companies signed up for one-on-ones with Blue Lagoon, a then pre-revenue gold miner with a $70 million market capitalization. With some of the industry's largest commercial gold miners meeting with Blue Lagoon and stating that they are interested in the 18-kilometer unexplored quartz alkaline resource, it leads us to believe there are orders of magnitude of potential to be explored in the years ahead.

The excitement around Blue Lagoon is in its potential resource scale. Until the drilling and exploration are done, we cannot accurately assess how large the opportunity is. What we see in the investments by the institutions above is expert insight into the geology and offtake, which could be massive. This upside we view as a free lottery ticket that will be drawn in the coming years.

Operational Milestone:

With the first revenue announcement, we anticipate greater clarity on the mining operations whose cash flows could drive a $210 million market capitalization. Separately, a second valuation driver will be Blue Lagoon's resource exploration of its 18-kilometer strike and other unexplored parts of Blue Lagoon's resource package. With the cash flows generated by its mining operations, Blue Lagoon plans to allocate half its cash flows evenly split between “infill” and “exploration” drilling. Once the company has filled its treasury and invested about $2 million on resource development capital expenditures, the company anticipates commencing drilling in June 2026.

The chart below of the Dome Mountain Property shows a golden-colored ellipse at the bottom of the map where the Boulder Vein is located. Significant drilling has occurred in this area since the February 2022 NI 43-101 was published and showed 225,000 Measured and Indicated ounces of gold. Over the next 12 months, the targeted one million ounces of gold could be proven in the Boulder Vein System area and drive a steady rise in BLAGF's resource valuation until that drilling plan completes. This infill resource development will drill additional holes to provide additional gold intercepts to meet regulations that certify whether a particular area qualifies as “inferred, indicated, or measured.” This infill drilling will provide Blue Lagoon with the data to plan a second mine for 2027, which will increase cash flows from operations and increase funding for future resource development in 2027.

Based on Michael White's assertion that historically operating company's gold “in the ground” is valued between $200/ounce and $300/ounce, we believe a resource valuation of $200-300 million market capitalization could be proven out over the next 12-18 months for this area.

The 25% of future cash flows that will be dedicated to resource exploration -- which is not infill drilling -- offers a higher valuation torque opportunity than infill drilling. That is because this exploration drilling could unmask Dome Mountain's true scale as an alkaline quartz gold formation associated with some of the world's most prolific resources.

“Dr. Hennigh draws similarities of the Dome Mountain geology to that of the Buritica Gold Mine in Colombia, South America. Although the deposit's footprint of 1.5 by 2.0 kilometers is smaller than Dome Mountain, there are many veins of high-grade mineralization that run vertically to depths of anywhere from 20 meters to 2,000 meters or more. Buritica, which is owned and operated by Xijin Mining of China, has reserves in excess of 10 million ounces of gold.

Other massive carbonate-type gold deposits include:

  • Porgera—Papua New Guinea—30 million oz.
  • Peñasquito Mine—Mexico 23 million oz.
  • Acupan—Philippines—14 million oz.
  • Fruta del Norte—Ecuador—12 million oz.
  • Blackwater—British Columbia—12 million oz.
  • Antamok—Philippines—11 million oz.
  • Hidden Valley—Papua New Guinea—10 Million oz.
  • Kelian—Indonesia—8 million oz.
  • Cowal—NSW, Australia—8 million oz.

What makes geologists such as Hennigh excited about the prospects for Dome Mountain is that it is a massive property of which 99% is still unexplored. Magnetic surveys indicate the potential for mineralization all over the property. ‘Crescat is very excited to help fund a very aggressive drill program at Dome Mountain this year, one that could result in the recognition of Dome Mountain as a potentially large, high-grade gold system,' commented Hennigh at the time of his initial involvement with the company last year.”

Valuation:

Blue Lagoon should benefit from three valuation drivers: mining operations, resource development, and rising gold prices. Since operations are just beginning and execution is unproven, our estimates should be seen as orders of magnitude markers.

  • Mining operations should see about $30 mm in cash flow in 2026. At a market cap multiple of 7 times cash flow, we believe mining operations could be worth $1.5/share. In 2027, with the addition of a second mine, cash flows and share prices could grow by an additional 50%. Below is a spreadsheet analysis showing the ramp of daily processing growing from 100 tons per day and rising to 200 TPD at year end and gold per ton rising from 6 grams of gold per ton to 12 grams per ton. Cash flow for 2026 is about $29,346,185. At 7x cash flow, BLAGF's market capitalization should be $205,422,875, and shares should be $1.46.

  • A resource development-based valuation, assuming one million ounces are proven via infill drilling in the Boulder Vein area in the next 15 months, could be $300 million, based on $300 per ounce of gold in the ground. With a share count of 140 million shares, a resource valuation per share could be $2.15/share. This resource valuation does not include exploratory drilling of the Chance Structural Zone or other nodes highlighted in the magnetic images shown above, which could expand the scale potential of the Dome Mountain resource.
  • The global debasement trade, coupled with foreign central banks buying record amounts of gold, suggests that capital markets have entered a bull market in gold. Given improving gold performance and central banks buying more gold than US Treasuries, higher gold prices are likely to improve mining cash flow valuations and resource valuations. Pierre Lassonde, gold billionaire and creator of the Lassonde Curve, recently said that a $6000/ounce (16:00 minutes) target on gold within three years is doable and $17,250/ounce (2:00 minutes) by 2032 is also possible.

Technical Analysis:

The stock chart below of Blue Lagoon Resources Inc. shows its previous high at $1.45 in 2020. In early 2020, gold miners were in vogue, and their valuations were higher than they are today; however, in the last five years, fundamental business development has occurred along with meaningful resource development. As such, a share price target of $1.40 is logical within the year, and, with operational and resource growth and at higher gold mining multiples, a price target that is multiples of the $1.40/share could develop in the years ahead.

The Gold Cycle:

Like Pierre Lassonde, we believe that there are cycles in the capital markets, and gold cycles are one facet of the global capital market. We believe that we are now in an inflationary cycle like the 1970s and the 2000 to 2011 period, where gold will significantly outperform the S&P 500.

The chart below from LongtermTrends shows protracted periods of inflation, in pink, where the ratio of the S&P 500 to the Producer Price Index declines. During the 1970s and 2000-2011 period, the S&P was basically flat, but gold rose about 10x. We believe that we have entered a period where gold could rise meaningfully as global currency debasement allows countries like the United States, Japan, Italy, and France to debase their massive deficits.

Stocks to Commodities Ratio

The Chart below, since 2010, shows the VanEck Junior Gold Miners ETF (GDXJ) is up 12%, the VanEck Gold Miners (GDX) is up 100%, SPDR Gold Shares (GLD) is up 286%, and iShares Silver Trust (SLV) is up 337%. At the precious metals peak in 2011, silver traded far above gold, and the VanEck Junior Gold Miners ETF (GDXJ) traded at a premium to gold (GLD) and the VanEck Gold Miners ETF (GDX).

If the past is prologue, then it seems reasonable that the precious metals market will continue to rise, and silver (SLV) and junior gold miners (GDXJ) could double or more over the next few years.

If the gold cycle remains engaged for the next several years (e.g., 2032 peak), then Blue Lagoon should be able to expand its exploration and then be bought out by a major gold miner seeking to generate gold mining cash flows on a district-scale high-grade gold mining resource whose entire scope is still unknown.

Risk Analysis:

Blue Lagoon is a high-risk stock because of its lack of liquidity and lack of profits. With an illiquid stock, one's ability to sell out of a position is very challenging, especially for positions greater than $100,000.

As shares rise in price and daily volume increases, liquidity improves. Many brokerage firms prohibit investors from buying companies like Blue Lagoon simply because their stock price is below a dollar or their market capitalization is below $100,000,000. The mere increase in liquidity, share price or market capitalization will allow more institutions to buy the shares or brokers to allow trading in BLAGF shares, which in turn increases the shares' liquidity.

With the first revenue announcement now public, Blue Lagoon's risk profile has begun to drop as it reports cash flow, mining results, and solid execution. Potential shareholders should not invest in positions which they cannot quickly sell. In fact, we see BLAGF as a publicly traded venture capital investment which investors could lose their entire investment.

Our assumptions are optimistic. We believe that the team will execute well and the cash flows will be delivered, but that is not a certainty. Our sense is that over time the potential massive resource opportunity will prove to be real, but that promising future is not a guarantee. This should probably be a small part of a retail portfolio, but high-risk hedge funds may want to move the needle with Blue Lagoon, but that would be a high-risk investment for a professional team managing risk capital for qualified clients. Blue Lagoon's great promise does entail risk, and investors should not invest more than they can lose, despite the shares' great promise.

Conclusion:

Blue Lagoon's recent 50% rise was triggered by receiving its first revenues. Recurring cash flows from mine operations and exploration should drive higher share prices to the $3/share range in the next two years. The real uncharted upside will be the exploration of the 18-kilometer strike of an alkaline quartz vein consistent with several district-scale resources, which we see as a “free lottery ticket.” Should Dome Mountain prove a world-class district-scale opportunity, the upside potential for BLAGF shares is massive.

With Blue Lagoon receiving its first revenues, a positive feedback loop of growing mining revenues funding resource exploration has begun. We don't know how long or how much growth will come from Blue Lagoon in the years ahead, but we are excited about the potential for its resource due to the afore mentioned institutional interest in Blue Lagoon. In the meantime, there appear to be several valuation drivers which should drive high equity returns for Blue Lagoon Resources in the next year or two.
